data.title: Vietnamese Authorities Uncover Massive Cryptocurrency Scam
data.description: Vietnamese police have dismantled a cryptocurrency scam of unprecedented scale, involving billions of dollars. According to Foresight News, the operation led to the arrest of blockchain entrepreneur Vuong Le Vinh Nhan, also known as Eric Vuong, and six associates on March 26. They face charges of illegal possession of property and money laundering. Since 2018, the group allegedly created and sold fraudulent cryptocurrencies through a platform named ONUS, which catered to millions of Vietnamese users and became inaccessible on March 20. Authorities accuse Eric Vuong of promoting fake tokens, manipulating supply and demand, and adjusting prices. Over 140 individuals have been summoned for investigation.

data.title: Leveraged ETFs Target SpaceX and Anthropic IPOs
data.description:
U.S. asset management firms REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management are planning to launch leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs) linked to the yet-to-be-issued publicly traded common stocks of SpaceX and Anthropic. According to BlockBeats, these ETFs aim to capitalize on two of the most anticipated initial public offerings (IPOs) in the U.S. stock market this year.

Investors holding the T-Rex2x Long SpaceX Daily Target ETF and T-Rex2x Long Anthropic Daily Target ETF will receive returns equivalent to 200% of the daily gains of these companies once they go public. SpaceX and Anthropic are expected to be among the most eagerly awaited IPOs of 2026. SpaceX may file for its IPO within days or weeks, while Anthropic's IPO is anticipated to occur this year.

data.title: Futu Prioritizes Crypto Business as Strategic Focus
data.description:
Foresight News posted on X (formerly Twitter) that Futu has elevated its crypto business to a strategic priority. Steve, a representative from PantherTrade, stated that the company will not launch a standalone app in the short term, as PantherTrade serves as a foundational infrastructure for business-to-business operations rather than a traditional exchange product. It is designed to be a 'super account' gateway for all assets.

Regarding the role of crypto within Futu's overall strategy, Steve mentioned that while detailed business plans are made quarterly, he prefers to focus on the broader potential and the ability to exceed targets rather than specific numerical forecasts. He believes that globally, U.S. stocks are currently the most popular and high-quality assets, but crypto assets have the potential to rival or even surpass them in the future. Steve emphasized that under regulatory compliance, the trading volume of crypto at Futu and within the industry could potentially match or exceed that of U.S. stocks, highlighting significant growth potential.

data.title: Renren Skill announced the start of its public beta test, aiming to create an AI skill store designed specifically for Chinese users.
data.description: Renren Skill has announced the start of its public beta, aiming to create an AI skill store designed specifically for Chinese users. The platform is fully adapted to the Chinese environment, providing a complete one-stop experience for browsing, installing, learning, using, sharing, and creating. Renren Skill has established three tiered communities to meet the diverse needs of users from beginners to hardcore developers. Furthermore, its community ecosystem supports users in easily building their own skills, and a reward and monetization mechanism will be opened in the future.

data.title: Iranian Military Warns U.S. Troops in Regional Hotels May Be Targeted
data.description:
Iran's military has issued a warning that hotels housing U.S. troops in the region could become targets for attacks. According to PANews, Iranian Armed Forces spokesperson Shekarchi stated that when U.S. soldiers occupy a hotel, it effectively becomes an American establishment from their perspective. He questioned whether Iran should stand by while Americans attack them, suggesting that any retaliation should occur where U.S. forces are located.

On Thursday, Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi accused U.S. soldiers in Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries of using civilians as 'human shields.'

data.title: STOCKS | Chinese Stock Indices Rise as Market Sees Broad Gains
data.description:
Chinese stock markets experienced a positive shift as the three major indices opened lower but closed higher. According to PANews, the Shanghai Composite Index surged in the afternoon, climbing back above the 3,900-point mark, while the Shenzhen Component Index increased by over 1%. By the end of the trading day, the Shanghai Composite Index had risen by 0.63%, the Shenzhen Component Index by 1.13%, and the ChiNext Index by 0.71%. 

The combined trading volume of the Shanghai and Shenzhen markets remained below 2 trillion yuan for the second consecutive day, reaching only 1.85 trillion yuan, a decrease of 90.3 billion yuan compared to the previous trading day. Market activity was characterized by rapid shifts in hotspots, with over 4,200 stocks across the market experiencing gains.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Predicts ECB Rate Hikes in April and June
data.description: Deutsche Bank anticipates that the European Central Bank (ECB) will implement interest rate hikes of 25 basis points each in April and June. According to ChainCatcher, this forecast aligns with the bank's analysis of the current economic conditions in the Eurozone. The expected rate increases are part of the ECB's strategy to manage inflation and stabilize the economy.

data.title: Japan's Aegis Destroyer Completes Upgrade to Launch Tomahawk Missiles
data.description: A ceremony was held at the U.S. Navy base in San Diego, California, on Thursday to commemorate the completion of upgrades to the Japanese Maritime Self-Defense Force's Aegis destroyer, Chokai. According to Ming Pao, this marks the first time a Japanese escort vessel has been equipped with the capability to launch Tomahawk cruise missiles. Japanese officials emphasized that the current security environment is increasingly severe, and possessing the ability to launch Tomahawk missiles will enhance the deterrence power of the U.S.-Japan alliance.

data.title: US Treasury Secretary Denies Report on Tighter Oversight of Fed
data.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has refuted a Financial Times report suggesting increased Treasury oversight of the Federal Reserve. According to NS3.AI, Bessent dismissed the claims, referencing his extensive published commentary on Fed reform, which exceeds 20,000 words. The disputed report alleged that Bessent had considered elements of a Bank of England-style framework for the relationship between the Treasury and the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Greeks.live: $13 billion in BTC options and $2.12 billion in ETH options settle today.
data.description: Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks.live, posted on the X platform that today is the quarterly settlement day, with 68,000 BTC options expiring. The put-call ratio is 0.56, the maximum price threshold is $74,000, and the notional value is $13 billion. 370,000 ETH options are expiring, with a put-call ratio of 0.56, a maximum price threshold of $2,250, and a notional value of $2.12 billion. Approximately 36% of Bitcoin and Ethereum options are expiring. A large number of options were rolled over this week, peaking at over 40% of total open interest. Currently, Bitcoin trading activity is relatively low, with a primary term IV of 51% and Ethereum's primary term IV of 70%. The decline in RV has led to a continuous rise in VRP, with 15D VRP reaching nearly 20% in the first half of this week. Bitcoin's performance in terms of both price and activity in the first quarter of this year has been poor, reflecting a lack of market confidence.

data.title: The yield on Japan's 10-year government bonds rose to its highest level since 1999.
data.description: The yield on Japanese 10-year government bonds rose to its highest level since 1999. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Experience Largest Single-Day Outflow in Over Three Weeks
data.description: Investors pulled a total of $171.12 million from 11 U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs on Thursday. According to NS3.AI, this marks the largest single-day outflow in just over three weeks. This withdrawal comes after more than $2 billion in inflows were recorded between late February and mid-month.

data.title: Robert Kiyosaki Advocates for Investment in Physical Assets Over Printable Ones
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki, author of 'Rich Dad Poor Dad,' has expressed his investment preferences, emphasizing a focus on physical assets. According to PANews, Kiyosaki stated that he avoids investing in any assets that can be printed by governments, banks, or Wall Street. Instead, he chooses to invest in tangible assets such as gold, silver, Bitcoin, and Ethereum.

data.title: BTTC Cross-Chain Bridge Interface Receives Major Upgrade
data.description: The BTTC cross-chain bridge interface has undergone a significant upgrade, enhancing user experience with a smoother interaction design. According to ChainCatcher, the new design incorporates user feedback, allowing asset transfers to Ethereum via BTTC addresses without incurring additional cross-chain fees, only requiring gas fees. The process is fully decentralized, ensuring users have complete control over their assets, with private keys and asset information inaccessible to third parties.

data.title: Tensions surrounding Iran have impacted the bond market, pushing the yield on German 10-year government bonds to 3.087%, the highest level since 2011.
data.description: Tensions surrounding Iran have impacted the bond market, pushing the yield on German 10-year government bonds to 3.087%, the highest level since 2011. (Jinshi)

data.title: German 10-Year Bond Yield Hits Highest Level Since 2011 Amid Iran Tensions
data.description: The yield on Germany's 10-year government bonds has reached 3.087%, marking its highest level since 2011. According to Odaily, this increase is attributed to the heightened tensions in Iran, which have impacted the bond market. The geopolitical situation has led to fluctuations in the financial markets, influencing investor sentiment and driving yields upward.

data.title: UBS Suspends Redemptions in Euroinvest Fund Amid Liquidity Concerns
data.description: UBS Real Estate GmbH has announced a suspension of redemptions in its Euroinvest fund, valued at $469 million, for a period of up to 36 months. According to NS3.AI, this decision follows a surge in withdrawal requests that significantly depleted the fund's available liquidity. An investor notice dated March 26 states that redemption requests submitted after March 25 will be blocked, and no new shares will be issued during this period. The fund experienced a decline of approximately 9% over the 12 months leading up to February 2026, primarily due to the impact of rising interest rates on European property valuations.

data.title: Bhutan Transfers 123.7 BTC to New Address
data.description: The Bhutanese government has moved 123.7 Bitcoin, valued at $8.5 million, to a new address. According to NS3.AI, this transfer marks a significant movement of cryptocurrency by the government. The reasons behind this transfer have not been disclosed, leaving room for speculation about the government's intentions or future plans regarding its cryptocurrency holdings.

data.title: Polymarket Sees High Trading Volume on Iran Invasion Prediction
data.description:
A newly created wallet on Polymarket invested $200,000 in a prediction that the United States will not invade Iran by March 31, with an opening price of 86.7¢. According to Odaily, the current market price for this prediction is 83.5¢, and the event has seen a trading volume of nearly $24 million.

An Iranian senior official stated yesterday that there are no current arrangements for negotiations, and any dialogue plans seem unrealistic at this stage. Iran's assessment of the '15-point proposal' is that the agreement is one-sided and unjust, favoring only the United States and Israel. Additionally, U.S. President Donald Trump mentioned that substantial negotiations with Iran are ongoing, and a new negotiation 'deadline' will be announced.

data.title: South Korean Investor Donates 100,000 XRP to Seoul National University Hospital
data.description: South Korean investor Kim Geo-seok has made a significant donation of 100,000 XRP, valued at approximately 210 million won or $152,000, to Seoul National University Hospital. According to NS3.AI, the hospital plans to liquidate the XRP in accordance with its internal guidelines. The proceeds from this donation will be allocated to development funds for both the main hospital and the children's hospital.
